# Feature: Smart Collection Type Detection ‚Äî Content-Based with Extension Fallback

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-005
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

When importing a collection, detect its format from the **file content**, not just the filename. Today `CollectionsImportWizard` calls only `detectFormatFromFilename` ([CollectionsImportWizard.tsx:39-42](../../packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsImportWizard.tsx)) ‚Äî a Postman export not named "*postman*" or a Swagger JSON without "swagger" in the name defaults to `wave` and fails to import. Content-based detection already exists (`detectTransformer` in [transformers/index.ts:62-73](../../packages/core/src/utils/transformers/index.ts), with Swagger ‚Üí Postman ‚Üí Wave priority encoded in the registry) but is unused by the wizard. This feature wires it in, with filename detection as fallback, keeping the manual dropdown as the override ‚Äî and extracts a shared `detectFormatFromContent` helper that FEAT-006 mirrors for environments.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No data-model changes. New pure function contract:

### Entities & Attributes

- **`detectFormatFromContent(text: string): ImportFormatType | undefined`** (new, in `transformers/index.ts`):
  1. `JSON.parse(text)` succeeds ‚Üí `detectTransformer(parsed)?.formatType` (registry priority: swagger ‚Üí postman ‚Üí wave; returns `undefined` if no `canHandle` matches ‚Äî do **not** default to wave here).
  2. Not JSON ‚Üí YAML heuristic for OpenAPI: a line matching `/^\s*(openapi|swagger)\s*:/m` ‚Üí `'swagger'`.
  3. Otherwise `httpFileTransformer.canHandle(text)` ‚Üí `'http'`.
  4. Else `undefined` (inconclusive).
- **Wizard detection chain**: `detectFormatFromContent(text) ?? detectFormatFromFilename(name) ?? 'wave'`.
- **Wizard state addition**: `fileContent: string | null` ‚Äî the text read at selection time, reused at import (single read).

### Relationships

- FEAT-006's environment wizard mirrors this helper's shape for environment formats.

### Indexes & Constraints

- Manual dropdown selection always wins over detection (unchanged behavior ‚Äî detection only sets the initial value on file select).

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/utils/transformers/index.ts` ‚Äî new `detectFormatFromContent`
- `packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsImportWizard.tsx` ‚Äî read content on select, detection chain, helper text
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/transformers/index.test.ts` ‚Äî detection unit tests (extend or create)
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/CollectionsImportWizard.test.tsx` ‚Äî wizard behavior tests (extend or create)
- `docs/features/collections.md` ‚Äî note that import format is detected from content with manual override

## 4. Dependencies

- Benefits from **FEAT-002** (broadened `httpFileTransformer.canHandle`) for `.http` content detection, but works against the current `canHandle` too ‚Äî no hard dependency. FEAT-001's Zod schema can later strengthen `waveTransformer.canHandle` (out of scope here).

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: `detectFormatFromContent` shared util

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `detectFormatFromContent(text: string): ImportFormatType | undefined` to `packages/core/src/utils/transformers/index.ts` implementing the chain in ¬ß2 (JSON ‚Üí `detectTransformer`; YAML key heuristic ‚Üí swagger; `.http` via `httpFileTransformer.canHandle`; else `undefined`). Guard `JSON.parse` with try/catch; treat empty/whitespace input as `undefined`. Export from the transformers barrel (already re-exported through core's `index.ts`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: one fixture per format ‚Äî Swagger/OpenAPI JSON ‚Üí `'swagger'`; Postman collection JSON (with `info._postman_id`/schema URL) ‚Üí `'postman'`; Wave collection JSON ‚Üí `'wave'`; OpenAPI YAML (`openapi: 3.0.0`) ‚Üí `'swagger'`; `.http` text ‚Üí `'http'`; ambiguous/garbage cases ‚Äî random prose ‚Üí `undefined`; arbitrary JSON (`{"a":1}`) ‚Üí `undefined` (no transformer claims it) unless `waveTransformer.canHandle` claims it (assert actual registry behavior); empty string ‚Üí `undefined`. JSON priority: a file with both swagger and postman markers resolves by registry order (swagger first).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc with `@example`; note in the function docs that registry order defines priority.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- transformers` green.

### TASK-002: Wizard integration ‚Äî detect on file select, content first

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `CollectionsImportWizard.tsx`:
  - `handleFilesAdded` becomes async: read `file.text()` immediately, store in new `fileContent` state, set `collectionType` from `detectFormatFromContent(text) ?? detectFormatFromFilename(file.name) ?? 'wave'`. On read failure fall back to filename detection.
  - `handleImport` uses the cached `fileContent` (no second read); clear it in `handleFileRemoved`/`handleClose`.
  - Manual dropdown unchanged as override; update the dropdown helper text to `Detected from file content` (shown after auto-detection; keep neutral text before a file is chosen).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: RTL ‚Äî selecting a Postman-content file named `myapi.json` sets the dropdown to Postman; Swagger YAML named `spec.txt` sets OpenAPI/Swagger; garbage-content `.json` falls back to filename then `wave`; user override after detection is preserved through import (`onImportCollection` called with the overridden type); import passes the same content that was read at selection.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: `docs/features/collections.md` ‚Äî import section: format auto-detected from file content (extension as fallback), manual dropdown overrides.
- **Verify**: Manually import a Postman export renamed to `collection.json` ‚Äî wizard preselects "Postman JSON" and the import succeeds.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: Detection is best-effort and never throws (guarded parse); a wrong guess is recoverable via the manual dropdown; actual import validation errors surface through the existing import error path (and FEAT-001's Zod validation at the service boundary).
- **Logging**: N/A (pure UI/util; imported file content must never be logged).
